name: JonDash official addons
description: Official add-on modules for JonDash. In JonDash, add this repo as a module source (Admin -> Modules -> Manage sources) to browse and install. Modules update independently of the JonDash base app. Channels: this manifest (main branch) = stable; the beta branch = beta. See VERSIONING.md.

modules.name: Health monitoring
modules.description: Watches your services with HTTP, TCP, ping, DNS and certificate checks, records uptime and response times, and alerts by email or webhook when something goes down.
modules.notes: Monitoring now starts with the server instead of when someone first opens the dashboard, so a restart overnight no longer leaves your services unwatched until morning. The advanced "how often to look for due checks" setting has been removed - it never controlled how often anything was actually checked.

modules.name: Module template (for developers)
modules.description: FOR DEVELOPERS — a working example to copy when building your own module. Installs to modules/template; open MODULE.md in that folder for a full guide, and AI-PROMPT.md to have an AI build one for you. Safe to install, and safe to uninstall when you're done.
modules.notes: The template depends on nothing: no helpers and no background work, so copying it gives you a module that installs on its own. Adds a done toggle (showing a follow-up migration and a permission the code actually uses), and a much fuller guide - including a paste-in AI prompt that covers building, testing and verifying a module end to end.

helpers.name: Scheduler
helpers.description: Runs modules' declared background work on time, starting when the server starts rather than when someone opens a page.
helpers.notes: Published ahead of the modules that need it. Requires JonDash 1.5.0 — helper support does not exist in 1.4.x, so nothing on the stable channel can use it until 1.5.0 ships.
